nodes.label: AI for technical, rule-based tasks
nodes.grounding_span: The more 'technical' and definitively right/wrong skills are where I find it saves time

nodes.label: AI as time-saving resource generator
nodes.label_negative: Manual resource creation for knowledge retention
nodes.grounding_span: I use AI for things like SPaG worksheets, so that I can tailor a specific revision lesson

nodes.label: AI outsourcing of thinking
nodes.grounding_span: I am reluctant to outsource too much of my thinking, because I feel like it causes brainrot

nodes.label: AI use for efficiency in data management and reporting
nodes.label_negative: AI use for core teaching and assessment
nodes.grounding_span: I'm hoping that I can figure out ways to use it to streamline e.g. for data management, creating resources for EAL students, or for helping with report writing

nodes.label: AI-generated student work
nodes.grounding_span: when you ask a student to write a paragraph for homework with very strict instructions and they just write out what ChatGPT gave them

nodes.label: Teacher expertise and subject knowledge
nodes.grounding_span: it's really important for me to maintain and develop my subject knowledge

nodes.label: Student learning and progress
nodes.grounding_span: if I don't read any of my students' work, I can't adapt my teaching effectively to help them make progress

nodes.label: Personal creativity and enjoyment
nodes.grounding_span: I also quite enjoy making resources, so it's a task I don't want to give up completely

nodes.label: Academic integrity
nodes.grounding_span: the inability to trust the academic honesty of student work

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for specific resource generation
nodes.grounding_span: One thing I found satisfying was when I needed a creative writing exercise for students, and I was able to ask ChatGPT to write me a story with very specific criteria

nodes.label: Frustration with AI misuse by students
nodes.grounding_span: one thing I find really frustrating is when you ask a student to write a paragraph for homework with very strict instructions and they just write out what ChatGPT gave them

nodes.label: Concern about over-reliance on AI
nodes.grounding_span: this is going to cause serious problems for humanity in the long run because they do not often think to question the validity of the response

nodes.label: Preventative caution about cognitive decline
nodes.grounding_span: I've noticed that I'm not as familiar with the content I'm teaching and therefore don't feel as effective in teaching it

nodes.label: Deliberate hands-on resource creation for knowledge retention
nodes.grounding_span: I am deliberately creating as many resources as I can myself because it's really important for me to maintain and develop my subject knowledge

nodes.label: Task-specific delegation based on objectivity
nodes.grounding_span: The more 'technical' and definitively right/wrong skills are where I find it saves time
